articles.title: Israeli assault across Lebanon kills 16 people, wounds over a dozen more
articles.summary: Israeli assault across Lebanon kills 16 people, wounds over a dozen more Israeli drone and air strikes in southern and eastern Lebanon have killed 16 people, Lebanese officials said on Wednesday. At least 21 others, including paramedics, have been wounded in these attacks. Amongst the areas hit were a school, a number of residential areas and civilian infrastructure.  Since March 2, Israeli attacks on Lebanon have killed over 2,710 people and wounded more than 8,35o others. Additionally, 1.6 million people have been left displaced as a result of ongoing Israeli aggression. People gat

articles.title: Hamas calls on US, mediators to stop the escalating Israeli attacks in Gaza
articles.summary: Hamas calls on US, mediators to stop the escalating Israeli attacks in Gaza In a press statement, Hamas said the latest Israeli attacks waged on Gaza represent a "blatant violation" of the ceasefire agreement signed in October.  "The US administration and the guarantor states of the Sharm El-Sheikh agreement must take immediate action to restrain the fascist occupation and compel it to cease its aggression against innocent civilians in the Gaza Strip," the movement added.  On Wednesday, several civilians were killed and wounded in Israeli air strikes across the besieged enclave.

articles.title: Israeli forces carry out incursion in southern Syria
articles.summary: Israeli forces carry out incursion in southern Syria Israeli forces late on Wednesday carried out a raid in the western countryside of Daraa province, south Syria, according to the Syrian state news agency SANA. Eight military vehicles reportedly advanced toward a bridge in Wadi al-Raqad, located in the Yarmouk Basin, and subsequently deployed across the area.  The latest incursion comes less than a week before Israeli forces carried out a similar raid into the nearby town of Jamlah. articles.title: Hezbollah says it carried out 17 attacks on Israeli forces in Lebanon
articles.summary: Hezbollah says it carried out 17 attacks on Israeli forces in Lebanon Hezbollah said its fighters launched 17 attacks on Israeli forces operating inside Lebanese territory on Wednesday. In a statement, the group said it targeted Israeli soldiers and military vehicles in Qantara, Biyyada, Taybeh, Naqoura, Rishaf and Aita al-Shaab using drones and rockets. Hezbollah also said it struck Israeli military equipment, including a personnel carrier in Qaouzah and a newly established command centre in Qantara.

articles.title: Dollar weakens as investors bet on end to Iran war
articles.summary: Dollar weakens as investors bet on end to Iran war The US dollar fell against most major currencies on Wednesday as investors reacted positively to signs the US-Israeli war on Iran could move towards a resolution. The dollar index, which tracks the currency against six major peers, dropped 0.4 percent to 97.941 after earlier falling to 97.623. The index reached its lowest level since late February, before Israeli and US strikes on Iran triggered the current conflict. The euro rose 0.6 percent to $1.1755, while the British pound gained 0.6 percent to $1.36125.

articles.title: Iran says ports ready to support shipping in Strait of Hormuz

The statement, carried by Iran’s state news agency IRNA, came after US President Donald Trump paused the US military’s “Project Freedom” operation in the strait, citing progress in negotiations with Iran over the US-Israeli war on Iran.

articles.title: Oil prices fall as markets react to possible Hormuz agreement
articles.summary: Oil prices fall as markets react to possible Hormuz agreement Oil prices dropped sharply on Wednesday while stock markets rose globally amid hopes that the US and Iran may be moving towards an agreement that would allow shipping through the Strait of Hormuz to resume normally. Brent crude, the international benchmark, fell 5.8 percent to $103.54 a barrel after climbing above $115 earlier this week. The decline came after US President Donald Trump said the Strait of Hormuz could be “OPEN TO ALL” if Iran agrees to a reported deal, though he did not provide further details.

articles.title: Bahrain says suspects linked to IRGC arrested
articles.summary: Bahrain says suspects linked to IRGC arrested Bahrain’s interior ministry said authorities have arrested an unspecified number of people alleged to have “close links” to Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, with some facing espionage-related accusations. The announcement follows a decision by Bahrain in late April to revoke the citizenship of 69 people accused of supporting Iranian attacks against the country. Authorities alleged those affected had shown support for Iran and were “colluding with foreign entities”.

articles.title: Israeli strike in Gaza kills senior Palestinian police officer
articles.summary: Israeli strike in Gaza kills senior Palestinian police officer An Israeli air strike in Gaza has killed a senior officer in the Palestinian interior ministry, according to health officials. Medics identified the victim as Naseem al-Kalazani, a colonel in Gaza’s police force, saying he was killed when his vehicle was targeted near the al-Mawasi area west of Khan Younis. At least 17 other people were wounded in the attack, according to the officials.
